input.initialPrompt:
I have a clean macOS machine and want to start developing .NET MAUI apps
for iOS and Android. I can't run commands right now, but I need a complete
setup plan. Tell me exactly what I need to install, how to discover the
right versions, and how to verify everything works. Please provide the
official recommended installation sources.

data.content:
Here's a complete setup plan for .NET MAUI on macOS, from scratch:

---

## 1. Xcode (for iOS)

**Source:** Mac App Store or [developer.apple.com/xcode](https://developer.apple.com/xcode)

- Install the **latest stable Xcode** (currently Xcode 16.x).
- After install, run:
  ```
  sudo xcode-select --switch /Applications/Xcode.app
  xcodebuild -runFirstLaunch
  sudo xcodebuild -license accept
  ```
- This gives you iOS SDKs, simulators, and the Apple toolchain.

**Verify:** `xcodebuild -version` → shows Xcode and Build version.

---

## 2. .NET SDK

**Source:** [dot.net/download](https://dot.net/download) — download the **latest .NET SDK** (currently .NET 9). Choose the macOS **Arm64** installer if on Apple Silicon, or **x64** for Intel.

**Discover the right version:** The [MAUI release notes](https://github.com/dotnet/maui/releases) and [MAUI support policy](https://dotnet.microsoft.com/en-us/platform/support/policy/maui) page list which .NET SDK versions are supported. Always match the latest stable .NET SDK to the latest MAUI workload.

**Verify:** `dotnet --version` and `dotnet --list-sdks`

---

## 3. .NET MAUI Workload

Install via CLI (no separate download):
```
dotnet workload install maui
```

**Discover available versions:**
```
dotnet workload search maui
dotnet workload list          # shows what's installed
```

**Verify:** `dotnet workload list` → should show `maui` (and its sub-workloads like `maui-ios`, `maui-android`).

---

## 4. Android SDK, NDK & Emulator

The MAUI workload installs the **Android workload**, but you still need the actual Android SDK/NDK. Two options:

### Option A — Let .NET manage it (recommended)
On first `dotnet build` of an Android project, the SDK auto-downloads to `~/Library/Developer/Xamarin/android-sdk-macosx`. Or force it:
```
dotnet build -t:InstallAndroidDependencies \
  -f net9.0-android \
  "-p:AndroidSdkDirectory=$HOME/Library/Developer/Xamarin/android-sdk-macosx" \
  -p:AcceptAndroidSDKLicenses=true
```

### Option B — Android Studio (manual control)
**Source:** [developer.android.com/studio](https://developer.android.com/studio)

- Install Android Studio → open **SDK Manager**.
- Install: **Android SDK Platform** (API 35 or latest), **Android SDK Build-Tools**, **Android SDK Command-line Tools**, **Android Emulator**, **Android SDK Platform-Tools**.
- Set the environment variable:
  ```bash
  export ANDROID_HOME="$HOME/Library/Android/sdk"
  export PATH="$ANDROID_HOME/platform-tools:$ANDROID_HOME/cmdline-tools/latest/bin:$PATH"
  ```
- Accept licenses: `sdkmanager --licenses`

**Discover required versions:** Check your project's `$(TargetFramework)` (e.g., `net9.0-android35` → API 35). The [MAUI docs](https://learn.microsoft.com/dotnet/maui/get-started/installation) list minimum required API levels.

**Verify:** `adb --version` and `sdkmanager --list | head -20`

---

## 5. (Optional but recommended) JDK

MAUI bundles **Microsoft OpenJDK 17** via the android workload. If you need a standalone JDK:

**Source:** [learn.microsoft.com/java/openjdk/download](https://learn.microsoft.com/java/openjdk/download)

**Verify:** `java -version` → should show 17+.

---

## 6. IDE

Pick one:

| IDE | Source |
|---|---|
| **Visual Studio Code** + [C# Dev Kit](https://marketplace.visualstudio.com/items?itemName=ms-dotnettools.csdevkit) + [.NET MAUI extension](https://marketplace.visualstudio.com/items?itemName=ms-dotnettools.dotnet-maui) | [code.visualstudio.com](https://code.visualstudio.com) |
| **JetBrains Rider** (has built-in MAUI support) | [jetbrains.com/rider](https://www.jetbrains.com/rider) |

---

## 7. End-to-End Verification

```bash
# Create a new MAUI app
dotnet new maui -n HelloMaui
cd HelloMaui

# Build for iOS (requires Xcode)
dotnet build -f net9.0-ios

# Build for Android
dotnet build -f net9.0-android

# Run on iOS simulator
dotnet build -f net9.0-ios -t:Run

# Run on Android emulator (start emulator first)
dotnet build -f net9.0-android -t:Run
```

If all four commands succeed, your environment is fully configured.

---

## Quick Version-Discovery Cheat Sheet

| What | How to check |
|---|---|
| Latest .NET SDK | `dotnet sdk check` or [dot.net/download](https://dot.net/download) |
| MAUI ↔ .NET compat | [MAUI release notes](https://github.com/dotnet/maui/releases) |
| Required Android API | Your `.csproj` `TargetFramework` value |
| Required Xcode version | [MAUI docs](https://learn.microsoft.com/dotnet/maui/get-started/installation) per release |
| Installed workloads | `dotnet workload list` |
